description: Add E2E tests for error states in the side panel: failed plugin cards rendering, optimistic permission rollback on bridge failure, and connection state wipe behavior.

userStories.title: E2E test: failed plugin cards render in the side panel with error details
userStories.description: Test that when a plugin fails to load (broken tools.json, missing adapter, etc.), a FailedPluginCard renders in the side panel showing the specifier, error message, and Show more/less toggle for long errors.
acceptanceCriteria: A broken plugin causes a FailedPluginCard to appear in the side panel
The card shows 'Failed to load' header with AlertTriangle icon
The error message is displayed (truncated to 2 lines by default)
For long errors (>100 chars), a 'Show more' button appears
Clicking 'Show more' expands the full error, button changes to 'Show less'

userStories.title: E2E test: permission change optimistic rollback on bridge failure
userStories.description: Test that when a permission change fails at the server level, the side panel UI rolls back to the previous permission value and shows an error alert.
acceptanceCriteria: Change a tool permission from 'off' to 'auto' in the UI
If the bridge call fails, the Select reverts to 'off'
An error alert appears on the plugin card
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-error-states.e2e.ts (same file)

Context:
PluginCard.tsx `handleToolPermissionChange` at lines ~84-110 does an optimistic update:
1. Immediately updates the local plugin state
2. Sends `setToolPermission` via bridge
3. If bridge call fails, rolls back the state and sets `toggleError`

The bridge call sends `bg:setToolPermission` ‚Üí background ‚Üí WebSocket ‚Üí server `config.setToolPermission`.

To trigger a failure: disconnect the server while the permission change is in flight. Or: kill the server, attempt a permission change (bridge returns error), verify rollback.

Simpler approach: stop the MCP server, then try to change a permission. The bridge call will fail because the WebSocket is disconnected. The Select should revert.

Actually, when the server disconnects, App.tsx wipes all state and shows the disconnected screen. So we can't test optimistic rollback by disconnecting.

Alternative: The rollback is hard to trigger in E2E because the server-side `setToolPermission` always succeeds (it's just a config write). The failure would only happen if the WebSocket message fails to send.

This test may be better suited as a component test rather than E2E. For E2E, we can verify the positive case: permission change succeeds and persists in config.json.

Revised acceptance criteria:
- Change a tool permission via the Select dropdown
- Verify the config.json reflects the new permission
- Verify the MCP tools/list shows the updated description prefix
- Verify the UI Select shows the new value

Note: this positive path is already partially covered in side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts. Skip this story if coverage is sufficient.

Constraints:
- If optimistic rollback cannot be reliably triggered in E2E, document this as a known gap and skip.

userStories.title: E2E test: connection loss wipes transient state but preserves persisted state
userStories.description: Test that when the server disconnects, the side panel wipes transient state (search results, installing set, errors) but persisted state (accordion expand, theme) survives reconnection.
acceptanceCriteria: With search results visible, disconnect the server
Side panel shows 'Cannot Reach MCP Server' state
Reconnect the server ‚Äî side panel reconnects and shows plugin list
Search results are cleared (search bar empty, no Available section)
Accordion expand state is preserved (from chrome.storage.session)
